sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano have had a mixed run in their last 5 matches, securing 1 win, 2 draws, and 2 losses, showing inconsistency with a tendency for low-scoring games. Real Betis, on the other hand, are in better form with 4 wins and 1 loss, scoring consistently and often netting multiple goals per game. Betis also have a strong attacking presence with players like Antony and Cucho Hernández in fine form.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, both matches ended in draws (2-2 and 1-1), indicating tight contests where both teams tend to score. Historically, over the last 5 head-to-heads, Betis have a slight edge with 2 wins compared to Rayo's 1, but recent games suggest a balanced rivalry with frequent BTTS (both teams to score) outcomes.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano, sitting 13th with 17 points, have struggled offensively (13 goals scored, 18th in La Liga) but are solid defensively (16 conceded, 2nd least). Real Betis, 6th with 24 points, boast a better attack (25 goals scored) and decent defense (19 conceded), showing more balance. Betis' superior league position and goal-scoring form give them an edge.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Rayo, Florian Lejeune has been a standout in head-to-heads with a 7.34 average rating and 2 goals in the last 5 against Betis. For Betis, Antony (7.49 rating, 5 goals this season) and Pablo Fornals (4 assists) are in form and could exploit Rayo’s defensive gaps.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Rayo Vallecano’s high possession (54.2%, 5th in league) and ability to create chances (6.13 corners per game) might test Betis’ defense, but their poor conversion rate (30.6%) is a concern. Betis’ early scoring tendency (36.8% of goals in 1-15 mins) and ability to score in bursts could capitalize on Rayo’s recent inability to keep clean sheets (5 games without one).

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Betis’ better form, attacking prowess, and Sofascore’s 53% probability for an away win, they are favored to edge this contest. Rayo’s home advantage at Estadio de Vallecas and head-to-head resilience suggest a close game, likely with goals on both sides. A narrow victory for Betis or a draw seems the most probable outcome.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Rayo Vallecano and Real Betis at Estadio de Vallecas ended in a goalless draw. Rayo Vallecano dominated much of the play, controlling possession and creating several clear opportunities to score. Despite their dominance, they failed to convert their big chances, which could be a concern heading into the second half. Real Betis, on the other hand, showed resilience in defense but struggled to create meaningful attacking plays. The match remains finely poised, with both teams looking for a breakthrough.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Rayo Vallecano's first-half performance
halftimeSections.text: Rayo Vallecano displayed a strong performance in the first half, dictating the tempo of the game with significant control of the ball. They created multiple big chances but were let down by poor finishing. Their frequent entries into the final third and penalty area showed intent, but the lack of composure in front of goal has kept the scoreline level. Defensively, they were solid, winning a high percentage of duels and aerial battles, which limited Real Betis' attacking threat. If they can sharpen their finishing, they are well-positioned to take the lead in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Real Betis' first-half performance
halftimeSections.text: Real Betis had a more conservative approach in the first half, focusing on defensive organization rather than attacking flair. They struggled to create any significant chances, with minimal presence in dangerous areas. While they managed a few shots on target, their overall threat was limited. Defensively, they held firm under pressure from Rayo Vallecano’s persistent attacks, but their lower duel-winning rate suggests they were often second-best in physical battles. They will need to be more proactive in the second half to turn the tide.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw a clear shift in attacking momentum, with Real Betis starting on the front foot in the opening minutes. However, Rayo Vallecano quickly took control, dominating the majority of the half with sustained spells of pressure in the attacking third. Their momentum was particularly strong in several key phases, where they pushed forward consistently. Real Betis had a brief resurgence towards the end of the half, showing some intent, but it wasn’t enough to seriously trouble the opposition defense. Rayo Vallecano’s attacking persistence suggests they are more likely to create scoring opportunities after the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Rayo Vallecano, players like Álvaro García and Andrei Rațiu stood out in the first half. García was a constant threat with his positioning and expected contribution in attack, while Rațiu made impactful runs and created a big chance. Their involvement will be crucial in the second half. On the Real Betis side, Pablo Fornals showed flashes of danger with his shots on target and expected goals, while Cucho Hernández could be key if Betis manage to counterattack. Both teams will rely on these individuals to unlock the deadlock.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second-half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Given the first-half dynamics, Rayo Vallecano appears to have the upper hand heading into the second half. Their dominance in possession and chance creation should translate into goals if they improve their finishing. Real Betis will likely look to absorb pressure and hit on the counter, but they must be more clinical if opportunities arise. Tactical adjustments could play a role, with Rayo possibly pushing for an early goal and Betis focusing on tightening their defense. The match is set for an intriguing conclusion, with Rayo Vallecano favored to edge it.
